channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Congress leader Rajeev Shukla raises concerns over US Green Card policy changes affecting applicants
item.description: A recent policy announcement by the US administration has triggered concerns among Indian political leaders and overseas professionals regarding changes to the Green Card application process. Accordin...

item.title: Clare Rigden highlights standout new TV series including Maximum Pleasure Guaranteed and Spider-Noir
item.description: Television reviewer Clare Rigden has shared her latest picks for must-watch programs across several streaming platforms and Australian broadcasters. Leading the recommendations is Maximum Pleasure Gua...

item.title: Canada and Nigeria strengthen economic relations as bilateral trade crosses $3 billion
item.description: Nigeria and Canada don reaffirm their commitment to deepen economic and trade relations as bilateral trade between both countries don pass $3 billion last year. Canada’s Secretary of State for Interna...

item.title: Steve McQueen’s First New Car: The 1958 Porsche 356 Speedster and Its Racing Legacy
item.description: This article explores the history of Steve McQueen’s 1958 Porsche 356 A 1600 Super Speedster, a car widely regarded as the first brand-new vehicle the actor ever purchased and a key milestone in his t...

item.title: Close race emerges in Colombia’s presidential election ahead of first round vote
item.description: A new AtlasIntel poll released shortly before Colombia’s first-round presidential election on May 31 shows a tightly contested race between the leading candidates, with right-wing businessman Abelardo...

item.title: Starmer urges free access to Champions League final broadcast in UK
item.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer has publicly called on TNT Sports to make the upcoming UEFA Champions League final freely available to viewers across the United Kingdom. His remarks come after the...

item.title: Teodoro Defends AFP Operations in Negros Occidental and Challenges Claims of Civilian Deaths
item.description: Defense Secretary Gilbert Teodoro Jr. challenged human rights organizations to provide proof supporting claims that six of the 19 people killed during military clashes in Toboso, Negros Occidental las...

item.title: Police recover skeletal remains of missing woman in Cotabato City; son-in-law hunted
item.description: Authorities in Cotabato City recovered the skeletal remains of a 40-year-old woman identified as Arbaina Guiapar after she was allegedly killed by her son-in-law eight months ago. The remains were dis...

item.title: Avane Plastic Surgery Hospital Launches Specialised Cosmetic and Reconstructive Care Facility in Nairobi
item.description: Avane Plastic Surgery Hospital (APSH), a specialised facility focused on plastic and reconstructive surgery, regenerative medicine, cosmetic dermatology, wellness, and longevity treatment, has officia...

item.title: Orlando Pirates crowned 2025/26 Premiership champions after decisive win over Orbit College
item.description: Orlando Pirates have been crowned 2025/26 Betway Premiership champions after securing a 2-0 victory over Orbit College at Mbombela Stadium, finally ending a 14-year wait for the league title. The resu...

item.title: D.C. mayoral race highlights tensions over federal cooperation, policing, and city management
item.description: The race to become the next mayor of Washington, D.C. is intensifying, with seven Democratic candidates competing to replace Mayor Muriel Bowser in a contest shaped by complex relationships between th...

item.title: Dick Parry, Pink Floyd saxophonist, dies at 83
item.description: Dick Parry, the British saxophonist widely recognised for his collaborations with Pink Floyd, has died at the age of 83. His death was confirmed by David Gilmour, who paid tribute to his long-time fri...

item.title: Russell and Antonelli collide in Canadian Grand Prix sprint race in Montreal
item.description: The Canadian Grand Prix sprint race in Montreal delivered early drama as Mercedes teammates George Russell and Kimi Antonelli came to blows during an intense battle at the Circuit Gilles Villeneuve. R...

item.title: Supreme Court Dismisses North Cotabato Clerk for Falsified Civil Service Examination Record
item.description: The Supreme Court has dismissed a clerk of court assigned at the Regional Trial Court Branch 22 in Kabacan, North Cotabato after finding her guilty of serious dishonesty for allegedly falsifying her c...

item.title: Security problems, late diagnosis and funding shortages dey worsen Ebola outbreak for DR Congo
item.description: The latest Ebola outbreak for the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) don quickly become serious public health problem. On 17 May 2026, the World Health Organization declare am as Public Health Emergen...

item.title: Orbit relegated after debut PSL season as Pirates secure title and Chiefs end campaign with defeat
item.description: The final day of the Betway Premiership delivered a dramatic conclusion to the season, with relegation battles, title celebrations and top-eight qualification all decided simultaneously. Orbit College...

item.title: WHO World Health Assembly declares stroke a global public health priority, urging stronger prevention and care systems
item.description: The World Health Assembly (WHA) has, for the first time, adopted a resolution recognising stroke as a major public health priority, calling on countries to strengthen action across the entire care pat...

item.title: Ontario government becomes involved in discussions over the future of Brantford Municipal Airport
item.description: The article reports that the Ontario provincial government has become involved in the ongoing debate surrounding the future of the Brantford Municipal Airport. The issue has attracted attention from l...

item.title: LSK Demands Public Inquest Following Death of Kiambu Student in Police Custody
item.description: The Law Society of Kenya (LSK) has vowed to pursue justice after the death of 24-year-old Brian Njung’e, a student at Kiambu National Polytechnic, who reportedly died while being held at Kiambu Police...

item.title: Mwinyi directs Zanzibar public institutions to become self-sustaining and generate revenue
item.description: Zanzibar President Dr Hussein Ali Mwinyi amesema umefika wakati kwa taasisi za umma kuachana na utegemezi wa mishahara na ruzuku kutoka Serikali Kuu na badala yake kujitegemea kifedha kupitia uzalisha...
